blocks.text: Medical Condition related to Home Health
Homebound due to (i.e inability to leave home, leaving
home requires considerable and taxing effort and is
medically contraindicated or requires assistance/ DME):
Clinical findings or services required ins support to patient's home health eligibility:
Education for safe use of assistive device
The patient is a 69 year old male who presents for an evaluation of left knee.
Transition into care is described as the following:
The patient is transitioning into care from another physician and a summary of care was not provided.
This patient presents to clinic today with complaints of left knee pain. Pain is localized to the medial aspect of the knee. Pain
is sharp with activity and aching at rest. He has attempted therapeutic exercise but it was too painful to continue. He has
used nonsteroidal anti-inflammatories and they did not provide relief. He cannot walk more than 200 m at a time due to knee
pain. He has difficulty ascending or descending stairs due to the knee pain.
Head, ears, eyes, nose, and throat: Atraumatic and normocephalic.
Alert and oriented to person, place, time, and situation.
Skin: No open lesions, rashes or wounds. No café-au-lait spots. No dark or hairy patches present.
Vascular: Normal distal pulses all limbs. Capillary refill less than 2 seconds all limbs. No adenopathy present. No stasis
changes about ankles. Normal finger and toenails.
Neurologic: Cranial nerves II XII grossly intact. Light touch intact all limbs. Motor exam is 5/5 upper and lower extremities.
Gait pattern is antalgic toward the left bearing full weight.
Hip: Limb lengths are symmetric. Nontender over the greater trochanter. No flexion contracture present. Nontender about
either buttock, thigh or knee to palpation. Nontender about the lumbar spine. Negative straight leg raise.
Left knee examination shows that there is a flexible 5 degree varus deformity. There is medial joint line tenderness. No
Radiographs of the left knee were obtained and are reviewed with the patient. Medial compartment arthritis with a varus
deformity. There is osteophytes medially. No pathology in the patellofemoral joint or in the lateral compartment.
Left knee medial compartment arthritis. Severe. The remaining compartments are uninvolved.
Patient is advised in this setting he is a good candidate for partial knee replacement. We discussed the indications risks and
benefits. We discussed the recovery timeline.
In anticipation of surgery I am ordering a CT scan for the planning protocol for the Mako knee.
Reviewed HPI, History and ROS at today's visit.
